House Programme

Enjoy a specially curated selection of Chinese opera performances complemented with fine tea and dim sum

Presented by our very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe, under the guidance and direction of renowned Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera – the local form of Chinese opera or xiqu.

The popular showcase of songs, instrumental musical pieces and performance excerpts is specially curated by Law to demonstrate a range of traditional styles and a changing programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy and martial arts. The experience also includes narration by an expert moderator offering insight into the history of the art form.

The upcoming season features three new performance excerpts: “Pingsheng Comforts his Wife” from _Time to go Home_, “Theft in the Mountains” from _The Legend of The White Snake,_ and “Reconciliation” from _Muk Kwai-ying Routing Hongzhou._ There is also new selection of songs and instrumental pieces, offering a rich new encounter for each performance. (Please see below for [daily programme details](https://www.westk.hk/event/tea-house-theatre-experience#programme-and-performers).)

To recreate the atmosphere of Hong Kong’s early 20th-century tea houses, audiences are served traditional tea and dim sum during the performance – a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

However much you know about Chinese opera, culture, history or literature, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is a captivating treat for long-time fans and newcomers alike – join us and discover for yourself!

Details

Concessions

*   Half price for senior citizens aged 60 or above, people with disabilities and a companion, Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA) recipients, and full-time students. Ticket holders must present proof of eligibility on admission. Concessions are available on a first come, first served basis.
*   Concessions apply for HK$350 and HK$300 tickets.

Age Limit

Children under the age of 6 will not be admitted.

Remarks

The programme and cast of each performance are subject to change without prior notice.

Enquiries

**Ticketing Enquiries**

Cityline Hotline Booking: (852) 2111 5333,[cs@cityline.com](mailto:cs@cityline.com)(Monday to Friday: 10am to 7pm, except public holidays)

WestK Ticketing Enquiries: (852) 2200 0022,[ticketinfo@wkcda.hk](mailto:ticketinfo@wkcda.hk)

Adverse Weather Arrangements

If a black rainstorm warning or a typhoon signal no. 8 or above is in force three hours before the start of the event, the event will be cancelled.

Overview

Artistic Team

Programme

Programme and Performers

Menu

Details

House Programme

Enjoy a specially curated selection of Chinese opera performances complemented with fine tea and dim sum

Presented by our very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe, under the guidance and direction of renowned Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera – the local form of Chinese opera or xiqu.

The popular showcase of songs, instrumental musical pieces and performance excerpts is specially curated by Law to demonstrate a range of traditional styles and a changing programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy and martial arts. The experience also includes narration by an expert moderator offering insight into the history of the art form.

The upcoming season features three new performance excerpts: “Pingsheng Comforts his Wife” from _Time to go Home_, “Theft in the Mountains” from _The Legend of The White Snake,_ and “Reconciliation” from _Muk Kwai-ying Routing Hongzhou._ There is also new selection of songs and instrumental pieces, offering a rich new encounter for each performance. (Please see below for [daily programme details](https://www.westk.hk/event/tea-house-theatre-experience#programme-and-performers).)

To recreate the atmosphere of Hong Kong’s early 20th-century tea houses, audiences are served traditional tea and dim sum during the performance – a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

However much you know about Chinese opera, culture, history or literature, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is a captivating treat for long-time fans and newcomers alike – join us and discover for yourself!

Overview

Artistic Team

Programme

Programme and Performers

Menu

Details

House Programme

Enjoy a specially curated selection of Chinese opera performances complemented with fine tea and dim sum

Presented by our very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe, under the guidance and direction of renowned Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera – the local form of Chinese opera or xiqu.

The popular showcase of songs, instrumental musical pieces and performance excerpts is specially curated by Law to demonstrate a range of traditional styles and a changing programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy and martial arts. The experience also includes narration by an expert moderator offering insight into the history of the art form.

The upcoming season features three new performance excerpts: “Pingsheng Comforts his Wife” from _Time to go Home_, “Theft in the Mountains” from _The Legend of The White Snake,_ and “Reconciliation” from _Muk Kwai-ying Routing Hongzhou._ There is also new selection of songs and instrumental pieces, offering a rich new encounter for each performance. (Please see below for [daily programme details](https://www.westk.hk/event/tea-house-theatre-experience#programme-and-performers).)

To recreate the atmosphere of Hong Kong’s early 20th-century tea houses, audiences are served traditional tea and dim sum during the performance – a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

However much you know about Chinese opera, culture, history or literature, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is a captivating treat for long-time fans and newcomers alike – join us and discover for yourself!

Details

Concessions

*   Half price for senior citizens aged 60 or above, people with disabilities and a companion, Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA) recipients, and full-time students. Ticket holders must present proof of eligibility on admission. Concessions are available on a first come, first served basis.
*   Concessions apply for HK$350 and HK$300 tickets.

Age Limit

Children under the age of 6 will not be admitted.

Remarks

The programme and cast of each performance are subject to change without prior notice.

Enquiries

**Ticketing Enquiries**

Cityline Hotline Booking: (852) 2111 5333,[cs@cityline.com](mailto:cs@cityline.com)(Monday to Friday: 10am to 7pm, except public holidays)

WestK Ticketing Enquiries: (852) 2200 0022,[ticketinfo@wkcda.hk](mailto:ticketinfo@wkcda.hk)

Adverse Weather Arrangements

If a black rainstorm warning or a typhoon signal no. 8 or above is in force three hours before the start of the event, the event will be cancelled.

# Tea House Theatre Experience

Buy Tickets

![Image 5: time_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/time.a340a615.png)Date and Time

Friday

7:30pm

Saturday

2:30pm, 5:30pm

Sunday

2:30pm

![Image 6: duration_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/duration.e84f799b.png)Duration

Approximately 90 minutes

Venue

Tea House Theatre, Xiqu Centre

![Image 7: tickets_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/ticket.13997dce.png)Tickets (HK$)

$350 (Balcony), $300 (Stalls)

*   Ticket includes performance, tea and dim sum.
*   Vegetarian options available on request. Please email [ticketinfo@wkcda.hk](mailto:ticketinfo@wkcda.hk) or call [(852) 2200 0022](tel:(852) 2200 0022) at least three days before the show and provide your ticket details.

Tickets available via Cityline, POPTICKET and the Xiqu Centre Ticket Office.

Service Fee:

*   Cityline limited-time offer – Online Ticketing and Hotline Booking: Waiver of service fee
*   [Xiqu Centre Ticket Office](https://www.westk.hk/whats-on/ticketing/ticketing-information): No service fee

![Image 8: bag_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/basket.31718a3f.png)Package Discounts (HK$)

House Programme

Enjoy a specially curated selection of Chinese opera performances complemented with fine tea and dim sum

Presented by our very own Tea House Rising Stars Troupe, under the guidance and direction of renowned Cantonese opera artist Law Ka-ying, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is designed to introduce new audiences to Cantonese opera – the local form of Chinese opera or xiqu.

The popular showcase of songs, instrumental musical pieces and performance excerpts is specially curated by Law to demonstrate a range of traditional styles and a changing programme of works exploring tales of romance, comedy, tragedy and martial arts. The experience also includes narration by an expert moderator offering insight into the history of the art form.

The upcoming season features three new performance excerpts: “Pingsheng Comforts his Wife” from _Time to go Home_, “Theft in the Mountains” from _The Legend of The White Snake,_ and “Reconciliation” from _Muk Kwai-ying Routing Hongzhou._ There is also new selection of songs and instrumental pieces, offering a rich new encounter for each performance. (Please see below for [daily programme details](https://www.westk.hk/event/tea-house-theatre-experience#programme-and-performers).)

To recreate the atmosphere of Hong Kong’s early 20th-century tea houses, audiences are served traditional tea and dim sum during the performance – a delicious way to learn about the heritage of Chinese traditional theatre.

However much you know about Chinese opera, culture, history or literature, the _Tea House Theatre Experience_ is a captivating treat for long-time fans and newcomers alike – join us and discover for yourself!

Details

Concessions

*   Half price for senior citizens aged 60 or above, people with disabilities and a companion, Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA) recipients, and full-time students. Ticket holders must present proof of eligibility on admission. Concessions are available on a first come, first served basis.
*   Concessions apply for HK$350 and HK$300 tickets.

Age Limit

Children under the age of 6 will not be admitted.

Remarks

The programme and cast of each performance are subject to change without prior notice.

Enquiries

**Ticketing Enquiries**

Cityline Hotline Booking: (852) 2111 5333,[cs@cityline.com](mailto:cs@cityline.com)(Monday to Friday: 10am to 7pm, except public holidays)

WestK Ticketing Enquiries: (852) 2200 0022,[ticketinfo@wkcda.hk](mailto:ticketinfo@wkcda.hk)

Adverse Weather Arrangements

If a black rainstorm warning or a typhoon signal no. 8 or above is in force three hours before the start of the event, the event will be cancelled.

07.11.2025

# Freespace Jazz Fest: Marf & Winka @COLLAR

[Buy Tickets](https://venue.cityline.com/utsvInternet/FREESPACE06/eventDetail?event=54383)

![Image 5: time_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/time.a340a615.png)Date and Time

7 November 2025 (Friday)

7:15pm, 9:30pm

![Image 6: duration_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/duration.e84f799b.png)Duration

Approximately 1 hour and 15 minutes

Venue

The Box, Freespace

![Image 7: tickets_icon](https://www.westk.hk/_next/static/media/ticket.13997dce.png)Tickets (HK$)

$680 (Standing)

Tickets available from 3pm on 29 September 2025 (Monday) via Cityline website and mobile app.

Tickets available at the Xiqu Centre Ticket Office and via Cityline telephone booking hotline from 30 September. Availability is subject to online sales and may be limited or unavailable.

Maximum purchase of 10 tickets per transaction.

Service Fee:

*   Cityline Online Ticketing: $12 per ticket
*   Cityline Hotline Booking: $15 per ticket
*   [Xiqu Centre Ticket Office](https://www.westk.hk/whats-on/ticketing/ticketing-information): No service fee

“Special queuing arrangements" will be implemented for entry:

*   All areas are free-standing. Admission is based on the designated queuing number of each ticket.
*   Your queuing number will be printed on your ticket or displayed in the confirmation email. Queuing numbers are automatically assigned by the ticketing system upon completion of each transaction.
*   The “Queuing Area” will open 90 minutes before the performance, subject to on-site conditions. Ticket holders must queue in the designated area according to the order of their queuing numbers.
*   Ticket holders in the “Queuing Area” will be arranged to enter The Box in order, starting 45 minutes before the performance.
*   If you arrive after tickets holders have started entering The Box, your queuing numbers will no longer be valid. Please wait to enter The Box until ticket holders from the “Queuing Area” have entered.
*   The above arrangements may be adjusted based on on-site conditions. The West Kowloon Cultural District Authority reserves the right to modify entry arrangements without prior notice.

[Festivals](https://www.westk.hk/en/whats-on?eventCategories=festivals)

[Music](https://www.westk.hk/en/whats-on?eventCategories=music)

[Season 25/26](https://www.westk.hk/en/event/freespace-jazz-fest/marf-winka-collar)

[![Image 8: Freespace Jazz Fest 2025](https://webmedia.westkowloon.hk/music-JazzFest25-main-thumbnail-20250714.jpg?VersionId=6BFXIeKPKe5S5Yma4s5wxvnvv9qGcpp6) part of this event Freespace Jazz Fest 2025](https://www.westk.hk/en/event/freespace-jazz-fest)

Overview

Performers

Details

Experience two of Hong Kong’s youngest breakthrough performers up-close in The Box

Join us this November for the Freespace Jazz Fest debut of Marf Yau and Winka Chan – two charismatic young members of Hong Kong’s hottest girl group COLLAR.

Acclaimed for the power of their vocals and choreography, the duo blend energetic street style with sweet rousing ballads, breaking canto-pop norms.

The performance features music direction by renowned local pop producer Terry Tsui.

Performers

![Image 9: Marf Yau](https://webmedia.westkowloon.hk/styles/field_person_image/s3/portrait-marf-yau.png?VersionId=oMz75o6GttO_eJXAB0C2PTUmfJ9kFL1O&h=f55b4ec2&itok=KiuAlJUC)

Performer

Marf Yau

![Image 10: Winka Chan](https://webmedia.westkowloon.hk/styles/field_person_image/s3/portrait-winka-chan.png?VersionId=FdOqfDLmkzY6abyKZC2oE.f8YfN9c3ge&h=f55b4ec2&itok=z_yfeSfE)

Performer

Winka Chan

Performers: Marf Yau, Winka Chan

Music Director: Terry Tsui

Details

Concessions

*   10% discount for full-time students, senior citizens aged 60 or above, people with disabilities and a companion, wheelchair user and a companion, and Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA) recipients.
*   Ticket holders must present proof of eligibility on admission. Concessionary tickets are available on a first come, first served basis.

Age Limit

Children under the age of 6 will not be admitted.

Remarks

*   After the show has commenced, latecomers will not be admitted until a suitable break.
*   Prohibited items for entry: 
    *   Items larger than 38 cm x 30 cm x 20 cm, including but not limited to light boards, banners, signs or flags.
    *   Professional cameras, telephoto lenses, tablets, laptops, GoPro cameras, detachable lens cameras, detachable phone lenses, binoculars, or other recording devices.
    *   Tripods or selfie sticks.
    *   Glass bottles, aluminium cans, reusable water bottles or thermos flasks.
    *   Long umbrellas.
    *   Stools or folding chairs.
    *   Helium or metallic balloons.

*   House rules 
    *   Unauthorised photography, videography, or audio recording is strictly prohibited in the venue.
    *   All audience members must undergo bag checks before entering the venue.
    *   Smoking is prohibited in the venue.
    *   Food and drinks may not be consumed in the venue.
    *   Flash photography is not permitted in the venue.
    *   Ticket holders must comply with the house rules and regulations of Freespace.

*   The organiser reserves the right to make changes to the programme without prior notice.

Enquiries

**Ticketing Enquiries**

Cityline Hotline Booking: (852) 2111 5333, [cs@cityline.com](mailto:cs@cityline.com) (Monday to Friday: 10am to 7pm, except public holidays)

WestK Ticketing Enquiries: (852) 2200 0022, [ticketinfo@wkcda.hk](mailto:ticketinfo@wkcda.hk)

**Programme Enquiries**

[freespacejazzfest@wkcda.hk](mailto:freespacejazzfest@wkcda.hk)

Adverse Weather Arrangements

If a black rainstorm warning or a typhoon signal no. 8 or above is in force three hours before the start of the event, the event will be cancelled.
